name: 2019 EULAR/ACR Classification Criteria for Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
classification_rule: Entry criterion: ANA ≥1:80 on HEp-2 cells or equivalent positive test ever. Then score additive domains; ≥10 points AND at least 1 clinical criterion = classify as SLE. Only the highest-scoring item in each domain counts.

domains_clinical.label: Joint involvement (≥2 joints with inflammatory arthritis features)
Renal biopsy Class II or V lupus nephritis
Renal biopsy Class III or IV lupus nephritis

domains_immunologic.label: Anti-cardiolipin OR anti-β2GP1 OR lupus anticoagulant positive

todo: Encode full additive-scoring engine and mutual-exclusion logic (only highest item per domain counts) — Day 2.

references.title: 2019 European League Against Rheumatism/American College of Rheumatology classification criteria for systemic lupus erythematosus
references.authors: Aringer M, Costenbader K, Daikh D, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Current reference classification. Introduces ANA entry criterion + additive weighted scoring. Sensitivity 96%, specificity 93%. Improves on 1997 ACR and 2012 SLICC by explicitly handling competing explanations.

references.title: Systemic lupus erythematosus
references.authors: Kaul A, Gordon C, Crow MK, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Comprehensive Nature Primer on SLE covering epidemiology, mechanism, diagnosis, management, quality of life, and outlook.

references.title: 2019 update of the EULAR recommendations for the management of systemic lupus erythematosus
references.authors: Fanouriakis A, Kostopoulou M, Alunno A, et al.
references.why_it_matters: Current EULAR treat-to-target recommendations: hydroxychloroquine in all, glucocorticoid minimisation, early immunosuppression, belimumab/anifrolumab.

references.title: Systemic lupus erythematosus
references.why_it_matters: Highly cited NEJM review covering SLE immunopathogenesis, clinical heterogeneity, and treatment principles. Standard reference for the mechanism section of any SLE writeup.

references.title: Update on Lupus Nephritis: Core Curriculum 2020
references.authors: Parikh SV, Almaani S, Brodsky S, Rovin BH
references.why_it_matters: Core Curriculum review on lupus nephritis. ISN/RPS biopsy class stratification (II–V) drives treatment intensity; classes III/IV carry the highest score in the SLE classification criteria and strongly favour classification.
